raw_consumer_statement:
XX/XX/year> In XX/XX/year>, approximately {$1500.00} in unauthorized charges were made on my credit card ending in XXXX. The charges were for multiple Best Buy gift cards that I did not purchase or authorize. The gift cards were for XXXX XXXX and XXXX, which are used for online gaming and virtual reality platforms. I have no knowledge or reason to use these products. 

I am a middle-aged woman, and these purchases are inconsistent with my lifestyle and normal spending patterns. At the time of these transactions, I was out of the country. I have attached both my passport stamp and flight receipt to prove this, as well as a police report ( Case No. XXXX ). 

When I first reported the fraud, the charges were removed, and my account number was changed. However, shortly after, the disputed charges reappeared on my new account, and the case was closed without my knowledge. I requested that the case be reopened, but it was again closed on XX/XX/year>, without resolution. 

I have disputed these charges multiple times in good faith and provided all supporting evidence requested, yet the credit card company continues to hold me responsible for charges I did not make. This is in violation of my rights under the Fair Credit Billing Act.

What I Want : 1. Reopen my fraud case and conduct a full, fair investigation.

2. Permanently remove the {$1500.00} in fraudulent charges from my account.

3. Provide written confirmation that I will not be held liable for these unauthorized charges.
